markdownRemark.html:
Pop-up Selection Input Box – A form control for value selection from a large dataset that pops up a selection input box. When to use: When users need to select values from a large data list. Requires integration with a backend value set API and proper configuration of corresponding field properties.

Lov is a large dataset value set component refined from the Pro component library to meet mid-platform scenario requirements. Compared to the quick code component discussed in the previous chapter, the difference lies in caching and data volume, while many other properties are universal.

Due to the large amount of data, Lov only caches the current value set view (popup list) configuration. Although Lov supports configuring the DataSet data source mode via the options property, backend support for value set view configuration is still needed, and it is recommended to query the data source.

Basic Usage

Field Configuration

{
  name: 'code',
  textField: 'code',
  type: 'object',
  lovCode: 'LOV_CODE',
  lovPara: { code: '111' },
},
// Component Rendering
<Lov dataSet={this.ds} name="code" />

Click to pop up

As can be seen, the LOV popup contains a table, which makes displaying the corresponding data very flexible and varied. It also supports most properties and interactions of a table.

For more reference, see Lov demo.

Note: The LOV front end does not perform caching or pre-querying. Therefore, when querying, the backend needs to return the corresponding display fields and value fields. The component will combine objects and display them, with object field combinations linked through the bind field attribute.

Prerequisites for Use

Backend support is required for Value Set View Configuration (table column configuration, etc.) and Value Set Data Configuration (table row data information).

Value Set Data Interface

The value set data interface - rules within the component are similar to quick codes.

  1. url: '/common/lov/${lovCode}/' - supports global configuration changes.

Value Set View Configuration Interface

The value set view configuration is composed of LovConfigItems and other data processing flag properties.

In the LovConfigItem data structure, you can configure a series of information such as table headers in the popup dialog when Lov is clicked.

Each LovItem represents a column in the table. In the simple Lov example above, the value set view configuration of LovItems is configured with two columns.

// lovDefineUrl returned value set view data configuration
const lovDefineTemple = {
  code: 'LOV_CODE', // lovCode
  description: 'Code',
  lovItems: [
    {
      display: 'Code', // The name displayed in the table column header
      gridFieldName: 'code', // The key and name of the table column
      gridFieldWidth: 300, // Default column width
      gridFieldAlign: 'left', // Column text alignment
      gridField: 'Y', // Whether it is view configuration information
      gridFieldSequence: 1, // Column sequence number
      ...
    },
    {
      display: 'Description',
      gridFieldName: 'description',
      gridFieldWidth: 300,
      gridFieldAlign: 'left',
      gridField: 'Y',
      gridFieldSequence: 2,
      ...
    },
  ], // Table Column Information
  placeholder: 'Please select a quick code', // Lov input box placeholder
  title: 'Parent Quick Code', // Lov pop-up title
  textField: 'description', // Display field
  valueField: 'code', // Value field
  width: 710, // Table width
  lovPageSize: '10', // Default page size for Lov pop-up
};

The Relationship Between lovDefineUrl, lovQueryUrl, and lovCode

By configuring the above three properties at the DataSet level, you can complete the basic configuration of Lov.

  • lovCode: The quick code for the data of the Lov component. lovDefineUrl and lovQueryUrl will automatically generate their corresponding backend URLs based on lovCode.
  • lovDefineUrl: The URL for obtaining the Lov value set view configuration.
  • lovQueryUrl: The URL for obtaining Lov value set data (table row data) during queries.
const ds = new DataSet({
  autoCreate: true,
  fields: [
    {
      name: 'code',
      textField: 'code',
      type: 'object',
      lovCode: 'LOV_CODE',
      lovDefineUrl: code => `/test_define/lov/lov_define?code=${code}`, // View configuration interface customization
		lovQueryUrl: code => `/test_query/lov/dataset/${code}`, // Custom data query interface
    },
  ],
  ...,
});

Note: Attributes like lovDefineUrl and lovQueryUrl support either passing in single component properties or modifying global configurations for more information.

Batch Query Configuration for Lov Value Sets

The value set view configuration of Lov will only be requested once and then cached.

You can use the lovDefineBatchAxiosConfig and useLovDefineBatch properties in the global configuration to configure batch requests.

The value set views of Lov in multiple DataSets on the same page can be requested in batch through a single interface to optimize and reduce the number of requests.

import { configure } from 'choerodon-ui';

configure({
  // Configure the axios config. For batch request interfaces of the GET type, append the lovCode for batch view configuration queries to the interface URL.
  lovDefineBatchAxiosConfig: (codes) => {
    return {
      url: 'https://hzero-test.open.hand-china.com/mock/lov-views/batch2',
      method: 'GET',
      params: codes.reduce((obj, val) => {
        obj[val] = val;
        return obj;
      }, {}),
    };
  },
  // Whether to perform a batch view configuration query can be determined through the lovCode and field field information.
  useLovDefineBatch: (lovCode, field) => {
    return true;
  },
});

Tree-style Lov

Core Idea of Tree Implementation

LOV pop-up view consists of: Modal Table.

Core processing: The data configuration has a certain parent-child relationship, and the idea of data association is basically the same as the tree structure in Tree and Table.

Component Configuration

It is basically the same as the basic LOV, with the difference lying in the way the data is handled.

// DataSet.Field Configuration
{
  name: 'code',
  type: 'object',
  lovCode: 'LOV_TREE_CODE',
}

// Component Usage
// Example 1: When backend data can be recognized as a tree structure
<Lov dataSet={this.ds} name="code" />
// Example 2: When backend data cannot be recognized as a tree, manually set mode to 'tree'
<Lov dataSet={this.ds} name="code" tableProps={{ mode: 'tree' }}/>

Data Processing

LOV Tree Identification

If the treeFlag in the data returned by the value set view configuration interface lovDefineUrl is 'Y', it indicates that the LOV is tree-structured data.

If it is not possible to identify whether it is tree-structured through the data returned by the backend, you can set mode: 'tree' in tableProps to achieve this.

Backend Data Structure Types

The format of the data returned by the backend determines how to configure it on the frontend. Based on the backend data, it can be classified as flattened or nested.

  1. Flattened: Parent-child data association is based on keywords in the backend data.
  2. Tree Nested: Nested data directly contains child data internally.

Flattened Data

Key Points of Data Association

In flattened data, parent-child relationships are formed based on keywords in the backend data. There are two key points for data association:

  1. The LOV tree flag treeFlag is 'Y'.
  2. Configure idField and parentIdField in the value set view configuration interface's returned data to form parent-child relationships.
// lovDefineUrl returned value set view data configuration
const lovTreeDefineTemplate = {
  // Key: tree-related configuration
  // Tree Lov flag
  treeFlag: 'Y',
  // Information named 'id' in backend data is the unique primary key of the data (may not be 'id', follow backend data)
  idField: 'id', 
  // Associate data based on information named 'parentId' in backend data (may not be 'parentId', follow backend data)
  parentIdField: 'parentId', 
  
  // Other basic backend data for Lov
  code: 'LOV_TREE_CODE', // lovCode value
  lovItems: [...], // Table column information
	...,
};
Flat Usage Example

Taking Lov Tree Structure as an example, in the value set view configuration where lovCode is LOV_TREE_CODE, there are declarations for treeFlag: 'Y', parentIdField: 'parentId', and idField: 'id' in these three data fields;

const lovTreeDefineTemple = {
	...,
  code: 'LOV_TREE_CODE',
  description: 'Quick Code',
  lovItems: [...], // view configuration, such as table column information
  placeholder: 'Please select a function', // Lov placeholder
  title: 'Parent Function', // Popup title
  valueField: 'id',
  width: 710,
  lovPageSize: '10',
  treeFlag: 'Y',
  idField: 'id', // The field named 'id' in backend data is the unique primary key of the data
  parentIdField: 'parentId', // Associate data based on the 'parentId' field in backend data
	...,
};

Parent-level data is as follows: Backend data information for the parent "Organization Structure" with an id of 2 as an example.

const TreeMockRootRows = [
  {
    expand: true,
    functionCode: 'HR',
    id: 2, // Unique data index
    text: 'Organization Structure',
    url: null,
  },
  { other root node data...},
];

Child-level data is as follows: If a data item has parentId of 2, it means it is a child node under "Organization Structure."

const asyncTreeMockData = {
  rows: [
    {
      id: 7,
      text: 'Employee Management (react)',
      url: 'hap-core/hr/employee',
      parentId: 2, // The parent of this data is the data with id 2, which is "Organization Structure"
    },
    {
      id: 73,
      text: 'Organization Management',
      url: 'hr/org_unit.html',
      parentId: 2, 
    },
    { other child node data...}
  ],
  success: true,
};

The final rendering effect is as follows:

Nested Data

Key Points of Data Association
  1. The Lov tree flag treeFlag is 'Y'.
  2. In the configuration of Value Set Component's Dataset Configuration, the childrenField property declares which key in the interface data is used to associate child data.
Nested Usage Example

Taking the Table Tree Data as an example, the root node contains 3 sets of data, and the businessObjectRelationFieldList in its internal data structure represents its child data groups. We can use dataSet.toJSONData() to observe the data structure at this point.

In the case of this type of nested data structure, you only need to declare the associated child field for its childrenField within the optionsProps at the Field level.

{
  name: 'code',
    type: 'object',
    lovCode: 'LOV_TREE_CODE',
    optionsProps: (dsProps)=>{
    return {
      ...dsProps,
      childrenField: 'businessObjectRelationFieldList',// Array name of nested child data content
    }
  }
},

The final result is as follows:

Users can choose the front-end configuration scheme that best fits the back-end data structure according to different situations.

Related APIs

Global Configuration

  • lookupAxiosConfig: Configuration or hook for list value requests;
  • lovDefineAxiosConfig: Configuration or hook for returning LOV (List of Values) configuration requests;
  • lovQueryAxiosConfig: Configuration or hook for LOV data query requests;
  • lovTriggerMode: LOV trigger popup mode, options are icon or input;
  • lovTableProps: Global configuration for LOV tableProps; if both lovTableProps exist, they will be merged;
  • lovDefineBatchAxiosConfig: Hook for batch query configuration of LOV settings, see AxiosRequestConfig for details;
  • useLovDefineBatch: Whether to use batch query for LOV configuration;
  • ...

See more.

Component Hierarchy Properties

  • modalProps: Modal properties, with higher priority than view configuration;
  • tableProps: Table properties, with higher priority than view configuration;
  • noCache: Automatically re-query when using a modal;
  • ...

For more, please see.

The usage scenarios and methods of Lov are extremely flexible and varied. The currently available configurations in the component library support most scenarios. If you encounter any difficulties during use or integration, please feel free to contact us. This introductory tutorial ends here, providing a basic guide to commonly used Pro components. We will continue to improve it, and we welcome feedback to help us make further enhancements.

Frequently Asked Questions

Scenarios where echo fails: After querying Lov data, the bind field does not echo?

// fields configuration
{ name: 'code', type: 'object', label: 'Code Description', lovCode: 'LOV_CODE' },
{ name: 'code_des', type: 'string', bind:'code.description' },

Lov related configuration fields: valueField: code textField: description

Backend query returns data: { code: xxx, code_des: aaa }

Problem: The field names conflict with the Lov object field names. The bind field looks for description under the returned code object → undefined, so it shows empty. Change the code field to another name, e.g., codeLov.

Summary: Clarify the relationship between Lov-related fields and the fields returned from the backend to understand the data objects.

How to allow adding new options in a value set?

// Lov component configuration combo to achieve composite input values
<Lov name="xxx" combo />

Note: The Lov data structure is an object. The composite input value is only a string. The value set will automatically handle the corresponding composite value. You can process and construct the data object on the front end (or submit it to the back end) after the input value changes, in line with the Lov option structure.

Otherwise, there will be issues with display and echoing in the popup.

For example, constructing an input object on the front end:

// DS configuration
new DataSet({
    autoCreate: true,
    fields: [
      {
        name: 'code',
        textField: 'description',
        valueField: 'codeId',
        type: 'object',
        lovCode: 'LOV_CODE',
        required: true,
      },
      { name: 'code_code', type: 'string', bind: 'code.code' },
      { name: 'code_description', type: 'string', bind: 'code.description' },
    ],
    events: {
      update: ({ dataSet, record, name, value, oldValue }) => {
        console.log('Lov value change event', record, name, value, oldValue);
        // After entering xxx by default, the composite value is {description: 'xxx', codeId: 'xxx', __dirty: true}, filling in the textField and valueField
// You can construct the input object as needed and submit it to the backend for storage
        if (name === 'code' && value && value.__dirty) {
          record.set('code',{
            code: 'xxx_Code',
            codeId: 'xxx_Id',
            description: 'xxx',
          });
          // Submit to backend
          // axios.xxx
        }
      },
    },
  });

Contact Us

H-ZERO Platform:https://open.hand-china.com/home

Official website:https://open.hand-china.com/choerodon-ui/zh

Github:https://github.com/open-hand/choerodon-ui
